title: “This price was set by an algorithm”: News subscribers are surprised by a new line in their renewal emails
tldr: WSJ and others now disclose that subscription prices are set algorithmically, surprising and unsettling readers. A signal moment for transparency, trust, and how personalization collides with audience relationships.
why_fresh: new disclosure trend raising trust questions
angle: Write about how algorithmic pricing transparency reshapes the trust contract between publishers and audiences.

title: Search traffic has declined so much that some publishers are considering opting out of Google entirely
tldr: 'Google Zero' has effectively arrived, pushing publishers to weigh cutting off Google altogether. A structural shift in how content gets discovered and how media business models must adapt.
angle: Explore what a post-search content strategy looks like when Google stops sending traffic.

title: Media Briefing: Brand authority and trust are becoming publishers' newest AI assets
tldr: Publishers bet that editorial authority and trusted brands are their biggest competitive edge in an LLM world. Reframes owned reputation as a defensible AI-era asset.
angle: Argue that editorial authority is the new distribution advantage when LLMs mediate discovery.

title: When Antonio Sánchez lost his job, he started his own daily YouTube broadcast reaching tens of thousands
tldr: A laid-off anchor rebuilt Oregon's only Spanish-language news as a solo YouTube show now reaching tens of thousands. A vivid case study in human-centered, one-person media filling institutional gaps.
angle: Use this as a case study on how one credible human can out-serve a whole institution.

title: In Graphic Detail: The real impact of creators at the World Cup
tldr: A data breakdown of creators' measurable impact at the 2026 World Cup. Concrete numbers on how creator content drove attention at a global event.
why_fresh: fresh data on creator campaign impact
angle: Break down what the World Cup creator numbers reveal about earned attention vs paid.

title: Workers are interested in creative AI tools. But hardly any are using them
tldr: Adobe finds 90% of workers want creative AI tools but only 9% actually use them. A striking gap between AI hype and real adoption in creative work.
angle: Write about why the AI creative-tool adoption gap is really a workflow and trust problem.

title: 3 lessons from jumping on a stupid meme way too late (Perdue's '6 7' nuggets)
tldr: Perdue knowingly launched a product on a meme that peaked months earlier — and owned the cringe. A candid campaign breakdown on timing, self-awareness, and brand voice.
angle: Dissect when 'being late and admitting it' beats chasing a trend on time.

title: How Dum Dums owner Spangler created a candy empire built on nostalgia
tldr: Spangler carved a niche by leaning into the storied histories of legacy candy brands. A useful nostalgia-as-strategy case study in narrative and brand heritage.
why_fresh: solid but evergreen brand case study
angle: Explore nostalgia as a durable content pillar and how to wield heritage without feeling stale.

title: Lifestyle brand Stanley uses AI behind the scenes — but execs claim it won't touch ad creative
tldr: Stanley deliberately keeps AI out of its ad creative while using it operationally. A clear stance on human craft as a brand value in the AI era.
angle: Make the case for 'AI-free creative' as a deliberate brand signal of humanity.

title: 3 questions to ask before publishing AI-generated content
tldr: A strategic communications professor argues every AI-generated message must tie to a specific goal and audience. Practical governance guardrails against purposeless AI content.
angle: Turn these into a lightweight content-governance checklist for AI-assisted teams.

title: 5 Signs Your Financial Content Program Has a Credibility Problem
tldr: AI engines and buyers now reward named, credentialed experts, exposing credibility gaps in generic content. Signals a shift toward expertise and attribution as ranking currency.
angle: Argue that visible human expertise is becoming the core ranking and trust asset.

title: As athletes embrace owned media properties, they're pushing for more involved brand deals
tldr: NBA players are leveraging their owned media to demand deeper roles in brand partnerships. Highlights the power shift toward talent-controlled content and audiences.
angle: Explore what athlete-owned media teaches brands about ceding narrative control to people.

title: The Best Time to Post on LinkedIn in 2026: 4.8 Million Posts Analyzed
tldr: Buffer's analysis of 4.8M posts pinpoints optimal LinkedIn timing for engagement. Fresh benchmark data for editorial planning and distribution.
angle: Question whether 'best time to post' still matters more than consistency and format.

title: How to Train AI to Think Like You
tldr: A framework for turning your meeting transcripts into a personalized AI profile that writes and reasons in your voice. Practical take on scaling individual voice with AI.
angle: Test whether an AI trained on your voice preserves or dilutes what makes content human.

title: 6 контринтуитивных советов для бизнеса от основателя Dream Job
tldr: Dream Job founder Boris Kurbatov shares counterintuitive lessons: why competitors' failures help, hiring 'wrong' people, and trusting intuition. Contrarian founder thinking useful for narrative and positioning.
angle: Adapt these contrarian founder principles into an executive-positioning narrative angle.
